vBRIEFInfo.description: Story vBRIEF for #1821 (content-manifest v1 + verify:content-manifest gate -- Wave-1 shippability classification); first input to #1669 Wave 1 (distributable Deft).

plan.title: Content-manifest v1 + verify:content-manifest gate -- Wave-1 shippability classification

narratives.Description: Re-scoped from a Wave-2 installer denylist patch into the Wave-1 shippability audit for #1669. Classify EVERY top-level repository entry (every directory and file) into one of four buckets -- content (ships to consumers), engine (executable enforcement code), harness (repo-dev tooling that supports the engine), repo-dev (maintainer-only artifacts that never ship) -- in a structured data file conventions/content-manifest.json. Pair it with a deterministic gate (verify:content-manifest) that fails CI when a top-level entry is unclassified, when a classified entry no longer exists, or when a bucket value is invalid. This converts the brittle installer denylist into an allowlist-by-classification and is the prerequisite audit that #1875 (the content/ move) consumes.
narratives.ImplementationPlan: 1) Author conventions/content-manifest.json: schema {version, description, buckets[], entries[]} where each entry is {path, bucket, note, straddle?}. Enumerate every top-level entry under git control (directories + files); apply the C3 straddle policy for mixed-content entries. 2) Implement the gate TS-native in packages/core/src/content-manifest/ exporting evaluateContentManifest() with three-state exit (0 clean / 1 drift / 2 config error), mirroring evaluateRuleOwnership. Drift = unclassified top-level entry, classified-but-missing path, invalid bucket, or duplicate path. 3) Add CLI wrapper packages/cli/src/verify-content-manifest.ts + register in dispatch.ts (CLI_MODULE_VERBS + verify:content-manifest alias). 4) Add tasks/verify.yml content-manifest target + wire into check:framework-source. 5) Tests: core unit (vitest) covering all three exit states + straddle handling, plus a self-test that the real manifest is clean against the real tree. No Python oracle -- new gate is TS-native per the #1669 direction (Python is being phased out; the parity discipline applies to ported gates, not net-new ones).
narratives.UserStory: As a deft maintainer preparing the engine/content split (#1669), I want a structured, gate-enforced classification of every top-level repo entry so that the content/ move (#1875) has an authoritative source of truth for what ships, and so future top-level additions cannot silently drift into (or out of) the consumer deposit without a CI failure forcing a classification decision.

items.title: Author conventions/content-manifest.json classifying every top-level entry

narrative.Acceptance: Every top-level git-tracked entry (dir or file) appears exactly once with a valid bucket (content|engine|harness|repo-dev) and a note; straddle entries carry a straddle policy note.

items.title: Implement evaluateContentManifest gate (TS core) with three-state exit

narrative.Acceptance: packages/core/src/content-manifest/ exports evaluateContentManifest(); exits 0 clean / 1 drift / 2 config error; drift detects unclassified entries, missing paths, invalid buckets, duplicates.

items.title: Add CLI wrapper + dispatch registration + Taskfile target

narrative.Acceptance: node packages/cli/dist/bin.js verify-content-manifest works; verify:content-manifest alias resolves; tasks/verify.yml target exists and is wired into check:framework-source.

items.title: Tests (vitest) covering all states + real-tree self-test

narrative.Acceptance: Unit tests cover clean/drift/config-error and straddle handling; a self-test asserts the committed manifest is clean against the real top-level tree; task check passes.

references.title: Issue #1821: Content-manifest v1 + verify:content-manifest gate -- Wave-1 shippability classification
Issue #1669: epic: distributable Deft -- npm/pip CLI, unified deft surface, external workspace mode
Issue #1875: Execute the content/ move -- manifest-driven relocation (Wave-1 Story 2, depends on #1821)
